chapter_title.en: Chapter 31 – Satisfaction with the Judgement (of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/ 8)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/ 8)
text: قُلْتُ لَهُ بِأَيِّ شَيْ‏ءٍ يُعْلَمُ الْمُؤْمِنُ بِأَنَّهُ مُؤْمِنٌ قَالَ بِالتَّسْلِيمِ لِلَّهِ وَ الرِّضَا فِيمَا وَرَدَ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ سُرُورٍ أَوْ سَخَطٍ .

en.hubeali: From him, from his father, from Ibn Sinan, from the one who mentioned it,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws, said, ‘I said to himasws, ‘By which thing does the Momin know that he is a Momin?’ Heasws said: ‘By the submission to Allahazwj, and the satisfaction regarding whatever traspires upon him, from the joy or anger’.
en.sarwar: 12. It is narrated from him from his father from ibn Sinan from those whom he has mentioned from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “I (the narrator) asked him, (abu ‘Abd Allah, recipient of divine supreme covenant), ‘By what means can a believer know that he is a believer?’ The Imam said, ‘(He can find out) by means of his submitting himself to Allah, and agreeing with whatever happiness or sadness that he may experience.’”
